steps: S=div H + det JH = 0 (Keller for Id+H)
deg 2d-2: det J(H_d)=0 => H_d = (alpha R, beta R)
Poisson+homog: K=0 or R=ell^d with K=c ell^{d-1}
if K=0 and R not pure: R_x cannot cancel (div condition) => contradiction (G1e)
if R=ell^d: domain GL(2) sends ell->y => axis form
axis form feeds x-drop induction (crack_induction) => deg_x f <=1

theorem: Plane Keller leading forms are pure powers of linear forms: H_d proportional (G1a); codomain shear to (R,0) (G1b); Poisson {R,K}=0 forces pure power or K=0 (G1c); gcd criterion characterizes pure powers (G1d); non-pure cannot be repaired by H_{d-1} (G1e); pure powers reduce to axis via GL(2) (G1f). Machine-checked through d=5.
